securityFindings.description: Unauthenticated ent.resolvers.go queries exposed all data across households — FIXED: added auth + household scoping
AddExpense/AddIncome picked first household without user specification — FIXED: added householdID input + membership verification
Income account error swallowed in import.resolvers.go — FIXED: proper error handling
CSV column mapping indices not validated — FIXED: non-negative validation added

evidence: All 7 plan summaries (01-07) present with outcome=complete
CONTEXT.json decisions followed: double-entry accounting, ent ORM, gqlgen schema-first
stageReviews.notes: All plans implemented per spec. Scope matches plan goals.

evidence: CRITICAL fix: All ent.resolvers.go queries now require auth and filter by user household memberships
HIGH fix: AddExpense/AddIncome now require householdID and verify membership before proceeding
HIGH fix: Income account error in import.resolvers.go no longer swallowed (was `_, _ :=`)
MEDIUM fix: CSV column mapping indices validated as non-negative before ParseCSV
go build ./... clean after all fixes
go test -count=1 ./... all 22 tests pass after fixes

findings.description: int64-to-int casts in report resolvers are safe for household-scale amounts but noted for future audit

stageReviews.notes: All CRITICAL/HIGH security issues resolved. CSV validation added. Code quality acceptable.
